question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $70,000 on April 20?

fragility_points: Single-minute settlement increases sensitivity to short-lived volatility.
A sudden risk-off move or Binance-specific disruption could invalidate the cushion quickly.
key_assumptions: No macro, crypto-specific, or Binance-specific shock drives BTC/USDT down more than about 6% before settlement.
Current Binance spot and kline data are reasonable pre-resolution proxies for the named settlement surface.
main_logical_chain: Interpret the contract literally: Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close on Apr. 20 must be higher than 70,000.
Measure current cushion: BTC is trading around 74.7k, so No requires a drop of roughly 6.2% by settlement.
Use the outside view: a >6% five-day drop is plausible but not the default outcome given recent closes and current regime.
Trim below market because exchange-specific, minute-specific settlement preserves real tail risk.
payload.main_thesis: BTC is already trading materially above 70k, so the base-rate case favors Yes on Apr. 20 noon ET, though single-minute settlement and crypto drawdown risk keep the probability below the market's 88%.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
payload.source_quality_view: Primary source quality is high because Binance is the named settlement venue; secondary contextual quality is medium-high via CoinGecko; independence is medium and source-of-truth ambiguity is low-to-medium.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can move more than 6% in five days, and the contract resolves on a single Binance 1-minute close rather than a broader average.
strongest_supports: Binance spot and average-price readings during the run were around 74.7k, comfortably above the 70k threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes were mostly above 70k.
CoinGecko independently cross-checked BTC around the same price range.
payload.timing_relevance: The contract settles at noon ET on Apr. 20, 2026, so short-horizon price persistence matters more than long-run BTC fundamentals.
unresolved_ambiguities: Pre-resolution verification used Binance public API endpoints rather than the exact future rendered candle on the trading UI.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would cut the probability if BTC breaks lower toward 71k-72k, if a sharp macro/crypto shock emerges, or if Binance-specific pricing/operational issues appear.

key_assumptions: No major still-pending scheduled catalyst before Apr. 20 noon ET is likely to push BTC below 70k by itself.
Binance remains operational and representative at settlement.

Check whether a major scheduled macro catalyst still remains before settlement.
Conclude that absent a fresh shock, the current cushion supports a high-probability Yes view, discounted somewhat for one-minute settlement fragility.

quote_anchors: Yes if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 ET has a final Close higher than 70,000

strongest_disconfirmers: A roughly 6% BTC drop in five days is plausible in crypto.
This market is fragile to a single Binance one-minute print rather than a broader average price.

unresolved_ambiguities: No direct read on ETF flow or large-holder flow between now and settlement.
Magnitude of unscheduled shock risk in the final five-day window remains uncertain.

Conclude that Yes is still the most likely outcome, but exact-minute settlement keeps confidence below the market's extreme pricing.
